[Skip to content](https://generateblocks.com/generateblocks-1-5-dynamic-data-query-loops-image-blocks/#content "Skip to content")

![](https://generateblocks.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/05/query-loop-featured.jpg)

This is a groundbreaking release with tons of awesome new features that really take GenerateBlocks to the next level.

## Dynamic Data

![](https://generateblocks.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/05/dynamic-data-3-1024x456.jpg)

You can now apply dynamic data to our blocks!

This allows you to return dynamic content in your Headline and Button blocks, and even add dynamic links to them. Post titles, dates, meta, lists of categories, and much more can now all be displayed inside your Buttons and Headlines with only a couple of clicks.

In our Container block, we can apply dynamic data to your background images so you can use data like featured images and post meta as your backgrounds.

Being able to use dynamic data like this is incredibly powerful and leads us to our next set of features.

## Query Loops

![](https://generateblocks.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/05/query-loop-featured-1024x456.jpg)

Back in December of 2021, we made an announcement on our WP Show Posts site that we were going to discontinue the plugin and merge its functionality with GenerateBlocks. Well, it’s here now! This update includes a new Query Loop block that allows you to build complex lists of posts (of any post type) right inside the editor.

The beauty of this block is that it uses all of our own blocks that you already know and love. Display your posts in columns (or not) with our Grid block, and display all of your post data (titles, dates, excerpts) with our Headline and Button blocks.

Headlines and Buttons handle all of your dynamic content, but we were missing a way to display dynamic images. That leads up to our next new block!

## Images

![](https://generateblocks.com/wp-content/uploads/2022/05/dynamic-data-4-1-1024x422.jpg)

We needed an image block that does things the GenerateBlocks way to really polish off this release, so we built it! Our new Image Block should feel very similar to the core Image block, but with a GenerateBlocks twist.

Options like padding, margin, border radius, and borders are now available for your images. If you’re using GenerateBlocks Pro, you can now use options like Effects and Custom Attributes directly on your images.

For captions, we use our Headline block so you have full control over the styling of them.

And of course, you can build dynamic images that grab the featured image, author avatar, or even an image from your post meta!

## New Components

In order to pull this update off, we had to rebuild a lot of components throughout the plugin. Since we were overhauling the code anyways, we ended up improving almost everything GenerateBlocks-related in the editor.

### Color Picker Component

Our Color Picker Component has been completely rebuilt from scratch. We no longer rely on core components here – it’s all GenerateBlocks.

### Number Component

We built a brand new Number Component that can include presets to save you time. These presets are filterable if you want, and you can also completely bypass the presets by clicking the custom input icon. When you use the custom input we remember your preference so the next time you go to edit a similar option (font size for example), the custom input will show by default.

### Dimensions Component

Our Dimensions component has also been completely rebuilt. It now feels much better (less lag when fields are synced) and the syncing feature works a lot better (empty values are now synced).

## Inline Background Images

You can now choose to output your background image URLs directly in the HTML instead of in the external CSS. This allows caching plugins to preload your background images and even convert them to other image formats if requested.

## Panel Memory

In an effort to improve the overall usability of our blocks, we implemented a memory for which panels you have open between our blocks. For example, if you’re editing the Spacing inside the Headline block and you switch to another Headline block, the Spacing panel will be open and ready just where you left it in the other Headline block.

## Default Container Width

For our users who use GeneratePress, the default Container width is controlled using the Container Width option in the Customizer. However, users using different themes can now alter the default Container Width directly in the Settings area of our plugin.

## Full Changelog

- Fix: Error in FSE when adding a Container to the front page
- Fix: Color picker autocomplete when typing in color
- Fix: Inherit box-sizing on gb-inside-container in editor
- Tweak: Full code refactor in the editor

- Tweak: Require WordPress 5.6
- Tweak: Rebuild color component
- Tweak: Rebuild dimensions component
- Tweak: Rebuild typography component
- Tweak: Add memory to open editor panels
- Tweak: Make device buttons sticky
- Tweak: Improve container width label
- Tweak: Use compiled assets in script registration
- Tweak: Improve button CSS selectors in the editor
- Tweak: Allow more decimal places in background image opacity

web.title: Dynamic Data Options Overview - Documentation (v1)
web.description: The Dynamic Data options allow you to dynamically call the data associated with your posts, pages, users and custom post types etc.
web.markdown: [Skip to content](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/#content) The Dynamic Data options allow you to dynamically call the data associated with your posts, pages, users and custom post types etc. ![](https://docs.generateblocks.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/2023-03-05_18-08-22.png) ## Data Source [Anchor link for: data source](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/\#data-source) Available in [Headline](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/headline-overview/) block, [Button](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/button-overview/) block, [Image](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/image-overview/) block and [Container](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/container-overview/) block. ![](https://docs.generateblocks.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/2023-03-05_18-11-20.png) Current PostPost type ### current post [Anchor link for: current post](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/\#current-post) When the Current post option is selected, it will output the data associated with the current post/page/custom post type. ### post type [Anchor link for: post type](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/\#post-type) It can be selected from posts, pages, products and custom post types as the data source. #### Select Souce Post [Anchor link for: select souce post](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/\#select-souce-post) You can select a specific post or leave it blank when using this option in a query loop block. ## Background Image Source [Anchor link for: background image source](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/\#background-image-source) Available in [Container](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/container-overview/) block. The selected image source will become the background image of the Container block. ![](https://docs.generateblocks.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/2023-03-05_18-32-31.png) featured ImagePost metaauthor avatar ### Featured image [Anchor link for: featured image](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/\#featured-image) This option sets the featured image as the background image of the Container block. The featured image is the image you have assigned in the post/page editor’s [Featured image](https://wordpress.com/support/featured-images/) panel. ### post meta [Anchor link for: post meta](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/\#post-meta) This option sets an image that is the value of a post meta as the background image of the Container block. Post meta refers to the additional information that can be associated with a post or page beyond the standard content. It is also known as the custom field. ### author avatar [Anchor link for: author avatar](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/\#author-avatar) Author avatar is a small image that is associated with a user account It can be uploaded/changed at WP dashboard > users > profile > profile picture. ## Image Source [Anchor link for: background image source](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/\#background-image-source) Available in [Image](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/container-overview/) block. ![](https://docs.generateblocks.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/2023-03-05_21-20-23.png) featured ImagePost metaauthor avatar ### Featured image [Anchor link for: image featured image](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/\#image-featured-image) This option outputs the featured image. The featured image is the image you have assigned in the post/page editor’s [Featured image](https://wordpress.com/support/featured-images/) panel. ### post meta [Anchor link for: image post meta](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/\#image-post-meta) This option outputs an image that is the value of a post meta. Post meta refers to the additional information that can be associated with a post or page beyond the standard content. It is also known as the custom field. ### author avatar [Anchor link for: image author avatar](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/\#image-author-avatar) This option outputs the author avatar which is a small image that is associated with a user account. It can be uploaded/modified/removed at WP dashboard > users > profile > profile picture. ## Content Source [Anchor link for: content source](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/\#content-source) Available in [Headline](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/headline-overview/) block and [Button](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/button-overview/) block. The selected content source will become the text of the Headline or Button block. ![](https://docs.generateblocks.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/2023-03-05_18-45-25.png)![](https://docs.generateblocks.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/2023-03-05_19-14-29.png) TitleExcerptPost datePost MetaComments numberList of termsAuthor meta emailNameNicknameFirst nameLast name ### Title [Anchor link for: title](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/\#title) This option outputs the title of the selected data source. ### Excerpt [Anchor link for: excerpt](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/\#excerpt) This option outputs the excerpt of the selected data source. ![](https://docs.generateblocks.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/2023-03-05_20-16-43.png) #### Excerpt Length [Anchor link for: excerpt length](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/\#excerpt-length) Define how many words you want to show in the excerpt. #### Use Default More Link [Anchor link for: use default more link](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/\#use-default-more-link) When this option is toggled, it will add a theme Read more link at the end of the excerpt. For example: ![](https://docs.generateblocks.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/2023-03-05_20-20-53-1024x125.png) ### Post date [Anchor link for: post date](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/\#post-date) This option outputs the date and time when a post/page/custom post type was published or last updated. #### Date type [Anchor link for: date type](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/\#date-type) You can choose from the published date and the last updated date. #### Replace with updated date [Anchor link for: replace with updated date](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/\#replace-with-updated-date) When this option is toggled, it will output the published date by default, if the post has been updated, it will output the last updated date instead. ### Post Meta [Anchor link for: content post meta](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/\#content-post-meta) This option outputs the value of a post meta. Post meta refers to the additional information associated with a post or page beyond the standard content. It is also known as custom fields or post custom fields. ![](https://docs.generateblocks.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/2023-03-05_20-34-07.png) #### Post Meta Field [Anchor link for: post meta field](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/\#post-meta-field) You can select the associated post meta from the dropdown list. If you don’t see a specific post meta in the dropdown list, ensure the meta is exposed to the REST API, or you can directly enter the meta field name into the post meta field. ![](https://docs.generateblocks.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/2023-03-05_20-35-55.png) ### Comments number [Anchor link for: comments number](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/\#comments-number) This option outputs the total number of comments. ![](https://docs.generateblocks.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/2023-03-05_19-04-04.png) #### No comment text [Anchor link for: no comment text](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/\#no-comment-text) You can define the output if there are no comments associated with the selected date source. #### Single comment text [Anchor link for: single comment text](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/\#single-comment-text) You can define the output if there is one single comment associated with the post. #### Multiple comments text [Anchor link for: multiple comments text](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/\#multiple-comments-text) You can define the output if there are multiple comments associated with the post. ### List of terms [Anchor link for: list of terms](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/\#list-of-terms) This option outputs a list of terms associated with the selected taxonomy. ![](https://docs.generateblocks.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/2023-03-05_19-07-25.png) #### Taxonomy [Anchor link for: taxonomy](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/\#taxonomy) Post comes with two default taxonomies: categories and tags. It can be chosen from the 2 taxonomies and other registered custom taxonomies. #### Term Separator [Anchor link for: term separator](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/\#term-separator) This option allows you to add a separator between terms. If you don’t want any, it should be blank. ### Author meta [Anchor link for: content author meta](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/\#content-author-meta) This option outputs the value of an author meta. Author meta refers to the additional information associated with a user profile. ![](https://docs.generateblocks.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/2023-03-05_19-22-49.png) #### Author Meta Field [Anchor link for: author meta field](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/\#author-meta-field) You can select the associated author meta from the dropdown list. If you don’t see a specific author meta in the dropdown list, ensure the meta is exposed to the REST API, or you can directly enter the meta field name into the author meta field. ![](https://docs.generateblocks.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/2023-03-05_19-25-04-1.png) ### Email [Anchor link for: email](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/\#email) This option outputs the email address associated with the selected user. ### name [Anchor link for: name](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/\#name) This option outputs the name associated with the selected user. ### nickname [Anchor link for: nickname](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/\#nickname) This option outputs the nickname associated with the selected user. ### First name [Anchor link for: first name](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/\#first-name) This option outputs the first name associated with the selected user. ### last name [Anchor link for: last name](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/\#last-name) This option outputs the last name associated with the selected user. ## Link Source [Anchor link for: link source](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/\#link-source) Available in [Headline](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/headline-overview/) block, [Button](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/button-overview/) block, and [Image](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/image-overview/) block. Available in [Container](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/container-overview/) block as [container link](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/container-links/) (Pro feature). ![](https://docs.generateblocks.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/2023-03-05_20-23-17.png) Single Postcomments areapost metaauthor archivesauthor metaauthor emailSingle Image ### Single Post [Anchor link for: single post](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/\#single-post) This option adds a link to the post. ### comments area [Anchor link for: comments area](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/\#comments-area) This option adds a link to the post’s comments area. ### post meta [Anchor link for: link post meta](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/\#link-post-meta) This option adds a link that is the value of a post meta. Post meta refers to the additional information associated with a post or page beyond the standard content. It is also known as custom fields or post custom fields. ![](https://docs.generateblocks.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/2023-03-05_20-33-05.png) You can select the associated post meta from the dropdown list. If you don’t see a specific post meta in the dropdown list, ensure the meta is exposed to the REST API, or you can directly enter the meta field name into the post meta field. ![](https://docs.generateblocks.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/2023-03-05_20-37-21.png) ### author archives [Anchor link for: author archives](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/\#author-archives) This option adds a link to the author archives which showcase all the posts that written by the selected user. ### author meta [Anchor link for: link author meta](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/\#link-author-meta) This option adds a link that is the value of an author meta. Author meta refers to the additional information associated with a user profile. ![](https://docs.generateblocks.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/2023-03-05_20-43-27.png) You can select the associated author meta from the dropdown list. If you don’t see a specific author meta in the dropdown list, ensure the meta is exposed to the REST API, or you can directly enter the meta field name into the author meta field. ![](https://docs.generateblocks.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/2023-03-05_20-41-34.png) ### author email [Anchor link for: author email](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/\#author-email) This option adds a link to the author’s email. ![](https://docs.generateblocks.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/03/2023-03-05_20-45-44.png) #### Link Type [Anchor link for: link type](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/\#link-type) Default: outputs the address of the email. Email: adds a prefix `mailto:` to the link. ``` johndoe@fakeemail.com ``` Telephone: adds a prefix `tel:` to the link. ### Single Image (image block) [Anchor link for: single image](https://docs.generateblocks.com/article/dynamic-data-options-overview/\#single-image) This option adds a link to the image file. Last updated on March 9, 2023

web.markdown:
![Thumbnail (1920x1080)](https://i.ytimg.com/vi/t9R_Qjq3UdY/maxresdefault.jpg)
# [GenerateBlocks Unleashes Dynamic Data and Advanced Layouts](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=t9R_Qjq3UdY)

**Visibility**: Public
**Uploaded by**: [David McCan](http://www.youtube.com/@DavidMcCan)
**Uploaded at**: 2022-06-22T14:04:27-07:00
**Published at**: 2022-06-22T14:04:27-07:00
**Length**: 13:11
**Views**: 9692
**Likes**: 237
**Category**: Howto & Style

## Description

```
The GenerateBlocks team just released version 1.5 of their Gutenberg plugin, and it is a game changer.  It unleashes dynamic data and advanced layouts making them available in the free version that can be used with any theme.  In this video I'll quickly show the differences between the previous version and the new version, and then look at the new Query Loop Block.  

There is a text summary of the video available on the WebTNG website:  https://www.webtng.com/generateblocks-1-5-unleashes-dynamic-data-and-advanced-layouts

Visit the GenerateBlocks website: https://www.webtng.com/go/generateblocks/
```

## Transcript

hi this is David mccan for web TNG in this video I'm looking at the new version of generate blocks the generate blocks team just released a new version of their Gutenberg plug-in version 1.5 and it's a game changer it unleashes Dynamic data and advanced layouts making them available in the free version that can be used with any theme in this video I'll quickly show you the differences between the previous version and the new version and then take a quick look at the new query loop block if you like the video please subscribe it helps to spread the word about the channel the free version of generate blocks is available in the WordPress plug-in directory you can see right now it's on version 1.5.1 okay I have a test site here with some demo data and a custom post type if we go look at the themes you can see right now I have the free version of the Cadence theme installed I do have the generate press theme installed and we'll come back to the generate press theme in just a minute if we look at the plugins you can see that I have the old version 1.4.4 of generate blocks activated okay and if we look at the dashboard we see that generate blocks the previous version had four blocks a container a grid a headline and a button Block in case you're not familiar with generate blocks it has a different take on building in Gutenberg rather than supplying a lot of pre-made blocks like info boxes or hero blocks it gives you the building blocks to put together your own presentation okay so it's a little more work but it's also very flexible now let's see what the previous version offered us in relation to Dynamic data let's go to a page and here I have a testing page and this is a generate blocks headline block and you notice there's no Dynamic data option now let's go and enable the premium version of generate press and this is the pro add-on for Generate press okay if we go back to our [Music] page we still don't see Dynamic data options however the premium version of generate press and the previous version of generate blocks does have some Dynamic data options if you're working with the elements feature this is a pro feature and let's go look at an element that I created previously a template plate for custom post type when you're working with the elements feature here we have the generate blocks headline block again but now you see a dynamic data option okay so these Dynamic data options in the previous version were available in this kind of theme and template building feature of generate press okay now let's go and update get the latest version of generate blocks let's go look at the dashboard and now we see that there are two more blocks there's the container the grid the headline and buttons but there's also a query Loop and an image Block in This ends the longest beta period we’ve experienced since the start of GeneratePress in 2014, and the result is the most powerful way to build WordPress sites. GenerateBlocks 2.0 Is Here - New Features, Better Performance, More Capabilities - YouTube [Photo image of GeneratePress](https://www.youtube.com/channel/UCoHk-JZFs1N-iI2DQlIKVnA?embeds_referring_euri=https%3A%2F%2Fgeneratepress.com%2F) GeneratePress 10K subscribers [GenerateBlocks 2.0 Is Here - New Features, Better Performance, More Capabilities](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=7uugkSjgqWg) GeneratePress Search Watch later Share Copy link Info Shopping Tap to unmute If playback doesn't begin shortly, try restarting your device. These changes have refactored how GenerateBlocks builds sites with new structures, allowing continued development and growth. GenerateBlocks 2.0 creates a new path forward, an exciting foundation that provides a streamlined and robust system to make building fast and effective sites easier than ever before. ## What’s New with GenerateBlocks 2.0 ### Bringing Professional-Grade Global Styles to Local Styles In GenerateBlocks Pro 1.7.0, we released our new Global Styles feature. This feature improved the usability of the GenerateBlocks Global Styles system, allowing anyone to build professional-grade code within the block editor. Bringing Global Styles to local styles was an obvious next step. Doing so was a monumental effort. The resulting work in GenerateBlocks 2.0, however, creates powerful tools for WordPress developers within the cleanest, fastest, and most stable website-building tool available. ### Performant Code Built for Fast Front-End Display GenerateBlocks builds your production-ready code as you build your pages. The CSS you generate using our styles builder is constructed, formatted, and minified as you click around inside the block editor. When you save your page, the CSS is already 100% production-ready for the front end of your site. GenerateBlocks builds the HTML for your site in the same way. As you develop your content, GenerateBlocks creates the front-end HTML code. In fact, you can see this happening as you do things like add a URL to a Button block. If you watch the HTML Attributes control (in Pro), you’ll see it is hard at work as you build your content. Absolutely no HTML or CSS variables or attributes require logic on the front end. Of course, dynamic content requires server-side rendering on a visitor’s request, but all supportive display code is prepared and ready to go. And for pages without dynamic content, the page is 100% ready for site visitor’s viewing. Even with zero coding knowledge, GenerateBlocks puts the power of professional-grade code into your hands. The result is the fastest and most performant experience available. ### Dynamic Content Puts Power Into Site Building Dynamic tags allow you to dynamically display site content anywhere inside block HTML code. Wherever the system finds these tags as the block renders, it will replace the tag with the defined dynamic content. ![Dynamic Tags in GenerateBlocks 2.0](https://generatepress.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/10/dynamic-tags-2.jpg) You can use these tags in your content, URLs, data attributes, and anything else that GenerateBlocks renders in the HTML. [See a full list of dynamic tags in our technical post](https://generatepress.com/generateblocks-2-0-a-new-era/). GenerateBlocks 2.0 dynamic tags can query post meta and deeply nested meta. If you have any meta that returns an array, you can access the data you need by specifying the keys. Even with complex arrays, dynamic tags can now make that nested information accessible for front-end display.  This works for post meta, user meta, and even the options table. Beyond the standard data fields in WordPress, you can also register dynamic tags yourself if you need something beyond the usual dynamic content. Using our API, you can register your own tags and completely control what they return. They’re also previewable inside of the editor by default. ### Streamlined HTML and CSS in Block Design In GenerateBlocks 2.0, we have decoupled HTML and CSS from the block structure. Previously, in version 1.x, HTML elements were often hidden within blocks. For instance, image blocks came with a permanent

# [How to Use the Query Loop Block in GenerateBlocks (Query Loop Series Part 1)](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=hQlIzKLZlHE)

**Category**: Education

## Description

```
In this tutorial, I'll walk you through the process of using the Query Loop block inside GenerateBlocks. We'll build a Query Loop from scratch, showcasing posts from a specific category on a regular WordPress page. This powerful feature allows you to dynamically display content tailored to your needs.

This is the first in a series of videos on getting the most out of the GenerateBlocks Query Loop block.  In our next video, we'll make the Query Loop even more dynamic by automatically pulling in posts from the current category. Stay tuned for more!

Learn More
GenerateBlocks: https://generateblocks.com/
GeneratePress: https://generatepress.com/
```
